results.title: Kinder Morgan AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Kinder Morgan has positioned itself as a key beneficiary of the AI boom, not through developing AI technologies but by providing critical energy infrastructure to power AI data centers[1]. The company has recognized that AI and data center growth represent a massive opportunity, with CEO Kim Dang describing discussions around natural gas demand for AI operations, cryptocurrency mining, and data centers as creating opportunities 'well in excess of 5 Bcf/d'[2]. The company's project backlog has grown dramatically to $9.3 billion by October 2025, driven primarily by natural gas projects aimed at serving LNG and data center demand[3]. Kinder Morgan operates about 79,000 miles of pipelines and transports approximately 40% of U.S. natural gas, positioning it strategically to benefit from
...
projected doubling of data center electricity consumption by 2030[4].
...

### Pipeline Infrastructure for Data Centers
...
Major natural gas pipeline projects to serve AI data center power demand across multiple regions
...
Artificial Intelligence (AI) and More Give Kinder Morgan a Robust Backlog
...
How does Kinder Morgan benefit from AI growth?
...
Kinder Morgan benefits from AI indirectly by providing the natural gas infrastructure needed to power AI data centers, which require massive amounts of reliable electricity that natural gas can provide 24/7.
...
What AI technologies does Kinder Morgan use internally?
...
The company uses Palantir AIP for pipeline operations optimization, Xage AI for cybersecurity insights, and has invested in Flyscan Systems for AI-powered pipeline monitoring.
...
Kinder Morgan is in commercial discussions for over 5 Bcf/d of power-related opportunities, including 1.6 Bcf/d specifically for data center demand, and projects 28 Bcf/d total U.S. demand growth by 2030.
...
What is Kinder Morgan's AI infrastructure investment strategy?
...
The company is investing $8.1+ billion in new natural gas pipeline projects specifically to serve AI data center power demand, while also deploying AI tools to optimize its own operations.
...

I graduated Cum Laude with a B.S. in Computer Information Systems and a minor in Business Administration and Management from the University of Houston in May 2025. My experience as a data analyst intern at Hapag-Lloyd allowed me to transform data into actionable insights, enhancing operational efficiency. Later, I was able to bridge technology and business as an IT Business Analyst intern at Trident 1, transitioning into a customer onboarding support role full time. Currently, I work as an Application Development Analyst at Kinder Morgan, Inc. With a solid background in data engineering and strong digital creative skills, including video editing in Adobe, I bring a unique blend of technical and creative expertise. I have a keen interest in project management and possess strong leadership skills, eager to contribute to team success.

results.title: Kristina S.
highlights:
Specialist-Scada Lead IT at [Kinder Morgan, Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/kinder-morgan)
...
### Specialist-Scada Lead IT at [Kinder Morgan, Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/kinder-morgan) (Current)
...
Technical lead SCADA developer for products and gas pipelines using primarily GeoSCADA and MSSQL software. Enhanced controllers experience by bringing down locks and speeding up the overall system in Geo and data transfer in SQL. Key projects: Lead technical design on migration project from Vector (Fortan) to Geo Scada (VBScript and C#). Designed, developed and integrated new common PowerShell controls to simplify and speed common daily tasks. (Power Shell 5&7 and SQL Server 2016-2022) Designed, developed, integrated, Power BI application that scrapes data from multiple servers to do analytics comparisons to highlight potential errors based on past troubleshooting issues during crashes and unexpected failovers. (Power BI 2022/25 and SQL Server 2016-2022) Introduced and helped design a standardized testing and deployment plan of change for the safety of the pipeline and control room. (DevOps) Troubleshoot via logs, event viewer, communication with vendors to discover software or server issues that arise and develop solutions for preventing situations. Worked 24hr on-call rotation to help with any unexpected emergency with any pipeline control room/servers. Show less
...

I identified the 𝗦𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗼-𝗧𝗲𝗰𝗵𝗻𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗹 𝗚𝗮𝗽 as the primary killer of AI ROI. When human definitions of success (e.g., "Net Revenue") don't match the raw database schema, even the most advanced LLM will suffer from 𝗠𝗲𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗰 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗮𝗱𝗶𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻. It isn't a lack of data that causes failure; it’s a lack of governed meaning.
...
𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗣𝗿𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗲𝗿 𝗩𝗶𝗲𝘄
...
As a leader, I saw this play out in the "Data Swamp." If Finance and Operations define a "Patient Encounter" differently, an AI Agent traversing a million tokens of raw logs will simply synthesize those conflicts into a single, cohesive-sounding lie. In healthcare, that isn't just a budget error; it's a liability.
...
𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁-𝗥𝗲𝗮𝗱𝘆 𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮 𝗘𝘀𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗲
...
To move beyond experimental pilots, we must move from "Data Access" to "Governed Activation." I’ve just published a full technical briefing on AlaminDataStrategy.com detailing the three architectural pillars required to solve this:
...
𝗠𝗲𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗰 𝗟𝗼𝗰𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴: Forbidding the AI from writing its own SQL.
...
𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗲𝘅𝘁𝘂𝗮𝗹 𝗠𝗲𝘁𝗮𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗮: Engineering the "Machine Rosetta Stone."
...
𝗗𝗲𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗰 𝗩𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: Installing the "AI Fusebox" circuit breakers.
...
𝗚𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗲𝗻𝗲𝗺𝘆 𝗼𝗳 𝗔𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗻𝗼𝗺𝘆; 𝗶𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗾𝘂𝗶𝘀𝗶𝘁𝗲.

Kinder Morgan (KMI) is a Oil and Gas company. Kinder Morgan is one of North America's largest energy infrastructure companies, operating approximately 79,000 miles of pipelines and 139 terminals. They transport natural gas, gasoline, crude oil, carbon dioxide (co. 2) and more. Their terminals store and handle renewable fuels, petroleum products, and other energy infrastructure solutions. Kinder Morgan employs 4,945 people (+5.4% YoY, +471 people) and has an annual revenue of $16.9B, founded in 1997. Headquartered in Houston, Texas, United States, operates in 13 countries (including Canada, India, France, Mexico, and Germany). Has a $40.7B market cap. IPO 2011.

results.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer at Jobgether - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
highlights:
# Senior AI/ML Engineer
...
We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
...
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems
...
Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development
...
Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.
...
- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.
...
- Contribute to the design and implementation of modern AI architectures, including RAG systems, LLM integrations, and agent-based workflows.
...
- Support database design and optimization, including writing efficient queries for large-scale data processing and retrieval.
...
- Translate complex machine learning and AI requirements into structured technical tasks and actionable development plans.
...
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.
...
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, or related field (PhD preferred).
...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
...
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.
...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
...
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
...
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
...
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.
...
- Nice to have: experience in the utility, energy, or power sector and strong Databricks expertise.
...
python artificial intelligence ai gcp gdpr aws cloud continuous delivery distributed computing mlops machine learning

# Lead Scientist, Artificial Intelligence Research
...
The Senior Engineer, AI is responsible for building AI and Machine learning models and pipelines, with a focus on generative AI, LLMs and predictive modelling. The successful candidate will work closely with data scientists and product leads to implement AI models, build required data and code pipelines through rigorous engineering disciplines You should have expertise in the design, development, management, and maintenance of systems and handling of large datasets. You should have hands on experience with common machine learning and AI frameworks, and a good knowledge of AI and machine learning fundamentals (deep learning, regression, classification and clustering algorithms), retrieval augmented generation pipelines, as well as a good grasp of the design and evaluation of LLM-supported use cases.

- Automate infrastructure used by the Data Science team
